evals.name: scrambled-mapping-four-pairs
evals.prompt: Convert this Coursiv column-matching exercise into the Markdown format we use for exercise notes: /Users/lairimia/VSCode/claude-code-skills/tmp/610cq.png and /Users/lairimia/VSCode/claude-code-skills/tmp/610ca.png
evals.expected_output: See /Users/lairimia/VSCode/claude-code-skills/tmp/610c.md for the target shape, BUT that reference file's column order does not match the unsolved screenshot's actual visual order (it follows the solved screenshot's row grouping instead) — verified against the images directly. Per user decision (same call made for the `prompt` skill's option-order discrepancy), the skill preserves the unsolved screenshot's original order, so this eval's column order and correct-matches mapping legitimately differ from 610c.md. Confirmed correct mapping from the images: 1→D, 2→C, 3→A, 4→B (left/right numbered per the unsolved screenshot's own order: left = Single big title, Three boxes in a row, Button centered under text, Section split into two columns; right = Call-to-action, Two-column layout, Feature grid, Header).
